sections.paragraph:
(Milêl) adv. downward, beneath, below, Deu 28:43 Pro 15:24. Opp. to מַעְלָה above. (This word has not sprung from מַטֶּה itself, but, as it appears from the acute penultima, from the shortened form מַט bending, a low place, with the addition of ה local.) With prefixes-

(1) לְמַטָּה-(a) downward, Deu 28:13 Eze 1:27 2Ki 19:30 Jer 31:37 Ecc 3:21.-(b) below, 2Ki 19:30 1Ch 27:23, “of twenty years old וּלְמַטָּה and under.” Followed by מִן Ezr 9:13, לְמַטָּה מֵעֲֹונֵנוּ “below our sin,” less than our sin deserved.

(2) מִלְּמַטָּה beneath (opp. to מִלְמַעְלָה above), Exo 26:24 27:5 28:27 36:29 38:4 39:20 .
